response.justification: Wei demonstrates exceptional technical depth across 15+ years of software development with expertise in high-performance systems and infrastructure. However, the resume contains zero evidence of people management or team leadership experience—the role explicitly requires a minimum of 2 years in people management as a core requirement for a full-time people-leadership position. The teaching workshops and mentoring activities shown are valuable but insufficient; they demonstrate technical guidance, not organizational management of reports or cross-functional teams.
strengths: Exceptional technical breadth: 15+ years across web, mobile, embedded systems, and infrastructure with demonstrated expertise in high-performance distributed systems handling millions of requests per second

Developer tooling and infrastructure background: GitLab/CI/Docker administration, testing frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), MCP code generators, and foundational experience with build/infrastructure systems

Teaching and knowledge-sharing track record: organized refactoring dojos, created workshops on CI/testing/web internals, published technical libraries and maintains open-source projects with real-world adoption
concerns: Complete absence of people management experience: no mention of managing reports, conducting performance reviews, mentoring individuals toward career goals, or running organizational teams—a stated minimum requirement
Developer tools background is infrastructure/DevOps-focused rather than compilers, automated release systems, or test automation frameworks specifically requested in the role

No evidence of organizational leadership: lacks demonstrated experience setting team priorities, developing mid-term roadmaps at scale, or aligning cross-functional teams—core manager responsibilities
